female_female_dialogue_examples:
JANE: No - Aunt, have pity -
MRS REED: And only when you are perfectly submissive will I let you out.
JANE: Please - I cannot bear it -
MRS REED: Silence. This violence is
JANE: I shall be killed -
MRS REED: Get back!
JANE: Have mercy, have mercy I beg you -

MRS REED: Who are you?
JANE: I am Jane Eyre, Aunt.
MRS REED: You. Is there no one in the room?
JANE: We are alone.
MRS REED: I've twice done you wrong. I broke the vow I made to Reed -
JANE: Please, don't think of it -
MRS REED: I am dying; I must get it out!

JANE: Who's there?
JANE: Who sleeps up here?
MRS FAIRFAX: No one. This part of the house is quite empty, except for you and me.
JANE: I heard someone.
MRS FAIRFAX: You can't have done.
JANE: A laugh. Someone laughed.
MRS FAIRFAX: Oh - that must be Grace Poole.

evidence_snippets: Jane is asleep in the heather, her shawl wrapped around her. A red-haired child in a white nightgown lies by her side, watching her. It is Helen Burns.
She sees a small red-haired girl in a white nightgown walking barefoot on the moors ahead of her. The girl turns,
A smiling lady dressed in black approaches her through the darkness - Mrs Alice Fairfax.
